steps.title: Determine the sphere radius from geometry
steps.content: The sphere touches the center of each face of the cube. Since the cube has edge length 82 inches, the cube's center is located 82/2 = 41 inches away from the center of each face. Therefore, the sphere's radius must be 41 inches.

steps.title: Calculate cube and sphere volumes
steps.content:
For the cube: $$V_{\text{cube}} = 82^3 = 551,368 \text{ cubic inches}$$
For the sphere, compute $41^3 = 68,921$, then: $$V_{\text{sphere}} = \frac{4}{3}\pi(41)^3 = \frac{4}{3}\pi(68,921) \approx 288,696 \text{ cubic inches}$$

steps.title: Find the remaining volume
steps.content: Subtract the sphere's volume from the cube's volume: $$551,368 - 288,696 = 262,672 \text{ cubic inches}$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
